title: Timeout Layers, Deadlines, Cancellation, Cleanup, and Budget Allocation · Browser and Node Client Sy… · Contract Lens: Cancellation Cleanup
summary: A search-as-you-type interface cancels stale requests with composed AbortSignals while preserving the winning result and announced status. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts cancellation cleanup increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch end-to-end request budget and cancellation model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Stop body processing, producers, timers, and queued work after cancellation and release every owned resource. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Allocate an end-to-end latency budget across attempts and phases while preserving time for cleanup and fallback. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Predict Cancellation Cleanup · Theory Cancellation Cleanup
steps.instruction:
theory case — TypeScript Fetch evidence model has 600 ms end-to-end for DNS, connect, TLS, headers, streamed body, parse, and cleanup. A caller cancels during body read; allocate phase budgets, propagate the reason, stop owned work, and preserve unknown remote effect. Competency probe: Stop body processing, producers, timers, and queued work after cancellation and release every owned resource. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Cancellation Cleanup contract for the supplied data.
